title: Integrating reportifyr into analyses
text: ’d like detail initialization reportifyr, please see vignette initializing reportifyr.
code: library(reportifyr) #> ── Needed reportifyr options ─────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ✖ options('venv_dir') is not set. venv will be created in Project root #> ℹ Please set all options for package to work. #> ── Optional version options ──────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ▇ options('uv.version') is not set. Default is 0.5.1 #> ▇ options('python.version') is not set. Default is system version #> ▇ options('python-docx.version') is not set. Default is 1.1.2 #> ▇ options('pyyaml.version') is not set. Default is 6.0.2  options("venv_dir" = file.path(here::here(), "vignettes")) initialize_report_project(file.path(here::here(), "vignettes")) #> Creating python virtual environment with the following settings: #>      venv_dir: /home/runner/work/reportifyr/reportifyr/vignettes #>  python-docx.version: 1.1.2 #>  pyyaml.version: 6.0.2 #>  uv.version: 0.5.1 #>  python.version: 3.10.12 #> Copied standard_footnotes.yaml into /home/runner/work/reportifyr/reportifyr/vignettes/report

what: Generating a figure for use with reportifyr
title: Integrating reportifyr into analyses
text: Let’s first create concentration-time plot Theophylline dataset:
code: library(ggplot2) library(dplyr)  data <- Theoph p <- ggplot(data, aes(x = Time, y = conc, group = Subject)) +   geom_point() +    geom_line() +   theme_bw() +   labs(x = "Time (hr)", y = "Theophylline concentration (mg/L)")  p

previous_headings: Generating a figure for use with reportifyr
what: Saving the figure and creating metadata
title: Integrating reportifyr into analyses
text: want include plot report ’ll need save figure ‘OUTPUTS/figures’ directory. two separate processes accomplish - easiest way call wrapper function ggsave_with_metadata: Alternatively call ggplot2::ggsave call write_object_metadata: processes give final result, however, severing tie saving object writing metadata may lead objects becoming sync. brings us metadata. reportifyr uses metadata create record object inserted word document aid reproducibility. reportifyr also uses meta_type parameter inject standard footnotes word document (vignette integrating reportifyr report writing). can use function get_meta_type see figure /table meta types currently saved standard_footnotes.yaml within ‘report’ directory upon initialization. just need provide path standard_footnotes.yaml want pull meta_types . Now, let’s re-save plot object conc-time-trajectories meta_type: Using meta_types also allows tab-completion help get meta_type correct! Let’s use preview_metadata_files see difference using meta type: also get_meta_abbrevs function see standard abbreviations can added: Let’s perform simple analysis computing subject level pharmacokinetic parameters study level statistics data set generate additional plot. ’ll compute maximum drug concentration (Cmax), time peak drug concentration (Tmax), area concentration-time curve (AUC) Theophylline data set performing linear regression weight AUC.  Let’s save plot logistic-regression-plot meta type: let’s view metadata files :
code: figures_path <- file.path(here::here(), "vignettes", "OUTPUTS", "figures") plot_file_name <- "theoph-pk-plot.png"  ggsave_with_metadata(   filename = file.path(figures_path, plot_file_name),    plot = p,   width = 6,   height = 4 ) ggplot2::ggsave(filename = file.path(figures_path, plot_file_name),                  plot = p,                  width = 6,                 heigh = 4) write_object_metadata(object_file = file.path(figures_path, plot_file_name)) meta_types = get_meta_type(file.path(here::here(), "vignettes", "report", "standard_footnotes.yaml")) names(meta_types) #>  [1] "logistic_plot"          "boxplot"                "TTE_plot"               #>  [4] "correlation_plot"       "GOF_plot"               "ETA_histogram"          #>  [7] "VPC"                    "kaplan-meier-plot"      "conc-time-trajectories" #> [10] "linear-regression-plot" "probit-regression-plot" "univariate"             #> [13] "final"                  "full"                   "alternative"            #> [16] "univariate_TTE"         "final_TTE"              "full_TTE"               #> [19] "alternative_TTE"        "ER_summary"             "covariate_descriptive"  #> [22] "frequency"              "probit-regression-fit"  "linear-regression-fit"  #> [25] "cox-regression-fit" figures_path <- file.path(here::here(), "vignettes", "OUTPUTS", "figures") meta_plot_file_name <- "theoph-pk-plot-mt.png"  ggsave_with_metadata(   filename = file.path(figures_path, meta_plot_file_name),    meta_type = meta_types$`conc-time-trajectories`,   width = 6,   height = 4 ) metadata <- preview_metadata_files(file_dir = figures_path) knitr::kable(metadata) meta_abbrevs <- get_meta_abbrevs(   file.path(here::here(), "vignettes", "report", "standard_footnotes.yaml") ) names(meta_abbrevs) #>  [1] "AUC"       "AGEBL"     "ALBBL"     "ALTBL"     "ASTBL"     "B2MBL"     #>  [7] "BSABL"     "BSTGBGE2"  "BSTGEQ3"   "CADC_21D"  "CADC_28D"  "CADC_42D"  #> [13] "CADC_56D"  "CATABL"    "CAVGA_21D" "CAVGA_28D" "CAVGA_42D" "CAVGA_56D" #> [19] "CAVGM_21D" "CAVGM_28D" "CMAXA"     "CMAXM"     "CNCEURO"   "CRPBL"     #> [25] "CYTORSK"   "ECOGBEQ2"  "ECOGBGE1"  "GLAUCBL"   "HEPFCH"    "HISTDIAB"  #> [31] "HSDRYEYE"  "HSIOSURG"  "IBMIBL"    "IGGBL"     "IGGFLG"    "KERABL"    #> [37] "LDHBL"     "LENSTABL"  "MEDFL"     "MMIGTY"    "MMLCTY"    "MMTY"      #> [43] "MPROTBL"   "PCD38"     "PRXGT3"    "PRXGT4"    "RACEBFLG"  "RACEWFLG"  #> [49] "RENALFCH"  "SBCMABL"   "SCH"       "SCHCH"     "SEXN"      "SPLITB"    #> [55] "STAGEBL"   "STRETCHB"  "TBILBL"    "WTBL" calc_auc_linear_log <- function(time, conc) {   auc <- 0      cmax_index <- which.max(conc)      for (i in 1:(length(time) - 1)) {     delta_t <- time[i + 1] - time[i]          if (i < cmax_index) {              auc <- auc + delta_t * (conc[i + 1] + conc[i]) / 2     } else if (i >= cmax_index && conc[i + 1] > 0 && conc[i] > 0) {              auc <- auc + delta_t * (conc[i] - conc[i + 1]) / log(conc[i] / conc[i + 1])     } else {              auc <- auc + delta_t * (conc[i + 1] + conc[i]) / 2     }   }      return(auc) }  pk_params <- data %>%   mutate(Subject = as.numeric(Subject)) %>%    group_by(Subject) %>%   summarise(     cmax = max(conc, na.rm = TRUE),      tmax = Time[which.max(conc)],      auc = calc_auc_linear_log(Time, conc),     wt = Wt %>% unique()   )  knitr::kable(pk_params) lr <- pk_params %>%    ggplot(aes(x = wt, y = auc)) +   geom_point() +   geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, se = TRUE, color = "blue") +   theme_bw() +   labs(x = "Subject weight (kg)", y = "AUC (hr mg/L)")  lr plot_file_name <- "theoph-pk-exposure.png" ggsave_with_metadata(filename = file.path(figures_path, plot_file_name),                       meta_type = meta_types$`linear-regression-plot`,                      plot = lr,                      width = 6,                      height = 4) new_meta <- preview_metadata_files(figures_path) knitr::kable(new_meta)

what: Generating a table for use with reportifyr
title: Integrating reportifyr into analyses
text: Now, let’s save pk_params data frame .csv can include report. can pass argument used write.csv well. Let’s use row.names = FALSE: Let’s also save Theophylline data set include report. can save .RDS file, just highlight functionality: end, ’ve generated two figures two tables saved alongside metadata using reportifyr, allowing us easily incorporate upcoming report writing!
code: tables_path <- file.path(here::here(), "vignettes", "OUTPUTS", "tables") outfile_name <- "theoph-pk-parameters.csv"  write_csv_with_metadata(   pk_params,    file = file.path(tables_path, outfile_name),   row.names = FALSE ) data_outfile_name <- "theoph-pk-data.RDS" save_rds_with_metadata(data, file = file.path(tables_path, data_outfile_name))

title: Initializing reportifyr
text: reportifyr loads message specifying options used set various values python setup. can choose leave options unset default values used instead. , comfortable setting values, can alter python virtual environment (.venv) created versions python, python-docx, PyYAML used.
code: library(reportifyr) #> ── Needed reportifyr options ─────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ✖ options('venv_dir') is not set. venv will be created in Project root #> ℹ Please set all options for package to work. #> ── Optional version options ──────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ▇ options('uv.version') is not set. Default is 0.5.1 #> ▇ options('python.version') is not set. Default is system version #> ▇ options('python-docx.version') is not set. Default is 1.1.2 #> ▇ options('pyyaml.version') is not set. Default is 6.0.2

title: Initializing reportifyr
text: first thing using reportifyr call initialize_report_project function. several things - creates needed directories saving output figures tables, sets ‘report’ directory, copies standard_footnotes.yaml file ‘report’ directory can used save footnote definitions. Additionally, sets needed python dependencies virtual environment python portion reportifyr package can properly function. ’ll need provide directory (typically project root directory) reportifyr look directories. directories found, initialize_report_project function create . Specifically, ‘report’ directory, well several sub directories, created found. ‘OUTPUTS’ directory, containing ‘tables’, ‘figures’, ‘listings’ sub directories, created exist. ‘.venv’ directory created, doesn’t exist, python-docx PyYAML saved directory. virtual environment initially created (.e., first time call initialize_report_project), reportifyr notify versions python, python-docx, PyYAML used virtual environment created. versions can set options running function displayed reportifyr loaded. directories files exist running initialize_report_project:
code: options("venv_dir" = file.path(here::here(), "vignettes")) initialize_report_project(file.path(here::here(), "vignettes")) #> Creating python virtual environment with the following settings: #>      venv_dir: /home/runner/work/reportifyr/reportifyr/vignettes #>  python-docx.version: 1.1.2 #>  pyyaml.version: 6.0.2 #>  uv.version: 0.5.1 #>  python.version: 3.10.12 #> Copied standard_footnotes.yaml into /home/runner/work/reportifyr/reportifyr/vignettes/report #> reportifyr/vignettes/.venv #> reportifyr/vignettes/data/images #> reportifyr/vignettes/OUTPUTS/figures #> reportifyr/vignettes/OUTPUTS/listings #> reportifyr/vignettes/OUTPUTS/tables #> reportifyr/vignettes/report/draft #> reportifyr/vignettes/report/final #> reportifyr/vignettes/report/scripts #> reportifyr/vignettes/report/shell #> reportifyr/vignettes/report/standard_footnotes.yaml

what: Create reproducible reports with quarto and word
title: Create reproducible reports with quarto and word
text: goal reportifyr assist semi-automatic report creation, providing streamlined process saving tables, figures, footnotes analysis scripts incorporating Microsoft Word documents report generation. reportifyr wrapper around officer R package python-docx python library, allowing users easily manipulate Microsoft Word documents R polished consistent reports.

title: Create reproducible reports with quarto and word
text: can install development version reportifyr like :
code: pak::pkg_install("a2-ai/reportifyr")

title: Create reproducible reports with quarto and word
code: library(reportifyr)

title: Create reproducible reports with quarto and word
text: starting reporting effort, important initialize reporting directories within project directory working . Please note development, directory structure names hard-coded. realize sub-optimal working allowing user-defined report/ OUTPUTS/ structure!
code: initialize_report_project(project_dir = here::here())

title: Create reproducible reports with quarto and word
text: take advantage advanced features reportifyr package (automatic abbreviation creation footnote placing) need capture metadata analysis. reportifyr package contains helpful functions easily capturing metadata. functions used actual analysis scripts. metadata .json files saved along side object files (figures tables) referenced building reports.
code: # Path to the analysis figures figures_path <- "OUTPUTS/figures"  # We can grab some standard meta types to ease footnote insertion meta_types = get_meta_types(project_dir = here::here())  # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # Construct a simple ggplot # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ library(ggplot2) g <- ggplot(data = Theoph, aes(x=Time, y = conc, group = Subject)) +    geom_point() +    geom_line() +    theme_bw()  # Save a .png using the helper function out_name <- "01-12345-pk-timecourse.png" ggsave_with_metadata(   filename = file.path(figures_path, out_name),    width = 9.4,    height = 6.72,   meta_type = meta_type$conc-time-trajectories )  # Or save with `ggplot2::ggsave` and immediately after run the `write_object_metadata` function out_name <- "01-12345-pk-timecourse2.png" ggplot2::ggsave(   filename = file.path(figures.path, out_name),    plot = g,   width = 9.4,   height = 6.72)  write_object_metadata(   file.path(figures.path, out_name),   meta_type = meta_type$conc-time-trajectories) # Path to the analysis tables tables_path  <- "OUTPUTS/tables"  # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # Construct a simple table # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------  # Let's save a .csv using the helper function out_name <- "01-12345-pk-theoph.csv" write_csv_with_metadata(   object = Theoph,    file = file.path(tables.path, out_name),   row.names = F )  # Or save with `write.csv` and immediately after run the `write_object_metadata` function write.csv(x = Theoph, file = file.path(tables.path, out_name), row.names = F) write_object_metadata(object = file.path(tables.path, out_name))

title: Create reproducible reports with quarto and word
text: basic example shows render Microsoft Word document figures, tables, footnotes. reportifyr also offers wrapper complete steps one function call:
code: # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # A minimal example # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # Load all dependencies. # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ library(reportifyr)  initialize_report_project(project_dir = here::here())  docx_in <- file.path(here::here(), "report", "shell", "template.docx") doc_dirs <- make_doc_dirs(docx_in = docx_in) figures_path <- file.path(here::here(), "OUTPUTS", "figures") tables_path <- file.path(here::here(), "OUTPUTS", "tables") footnotes <- file.path(here::here(), "report", "standard_footnotes.yaml")  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Step 1. # Table addition, running `add_tables` will format and insert tables into the doc. # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- add_tables(   docx_in = doc_dirs$doc_clean,   docx_out = doc_dirs$doc_tables,   tables_path = tables_path )  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Step 2. # Next we place in the plots using the `add_plots` function. # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- add_plots(   docx_in = doc_dirs$doc_tables,   docx_out = doc_dirs$doc_tabs_figs,   figures_path = figures_path )  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Step 3. # Now we can add the footnotes to all the inserted figures and tables using `add_footnotes`. # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- add_footnotes(   docx_in = doc_dirs$doc_tabs_figs,   docx_out = doc_dirs$doc_draft,   figures_path = figures_path,   tables_path = tables_path,   footnotes = footnotes )  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Step 4. # Clean the output for final document creation using `finalize_document`.  # This will remove the ties between reportifyr and the document so be careful! # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- finalize_document(   docx_in = doc_dirs$doc_draft,   docx_out = doc_dirs$doc_final ) library(reportifyr)  initialize_report_project(project_dir = here::here())  figures_path <- file.path(here::here(), "OUTPUTS", "figures") tables_path <- file.path(here::here(), "OUTPUTS", "tables") footnotes <- file.path(here::here(), "report", "standard_footnotes.yaml")  docx_in <- file.path(here::here(), "report", "shell", "template.docx") docx_out <- file.path(here::here(), "report", "draft", "draft.docx")  build_report(docx_in = docx_in,              docx_out = docx_out,              figures_path = figures_path,              tables_path = tables_path,              standard_footnotes_yaml = footnotes)               # If you're satisfied with the report and want to remove the magic strings, you'll  # need to run `finalize_document` finalize_document(docx_in = docx_out,                   docx_out = "report/final/final.docx")

what: Inserts Footnotes in appropriate places in Word files — add_footnotes
title: Inserts Footnotes in appropriate places in Word files — add_footnotes
text: Reads .docx file returns new version footnotes placed appropriate places document.

title: Inserts Footnotes in appropriate places in Word files — add_footnotes
code: add_footnotes(   docx_in,   docx_out,   figures_path,   tables_path,   footnotes = NULL,   include_object_path = FALSE,   footnotes_fail_on_missing_metadata = TRUE,   debug = F )

title: Inserts Footnotes in appropriate places in Word files — add_footnotes
text: docx_in Path input .docx file docx_out Path output .docx save figures_path Path images associated metadata directory tables_path Path tables associated metadata directory footnotes Path standard_footnotes.yaml include_object_path Boolean including object path path footnotes footnotes_fail_on_missing_metadata Boolean allowing objects lack metadata thus footnotes debug Debug

text: docx_in Path input .docx file docx_out Path output .docx save figures_path Path images file directory fig_width Figure width inches. global controller. Defaults NULL. NULL, size calculated calculated based pixels actual figure fig_height Figure height inches. global controller. Defaults NULL. NULL, size automatically calculated based pixels actual figure debug Debug

title: Inserts Figures in appropriate places in Word files — add_plots
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Load all dependencies # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- docx_in <- file.path(here::here(), "report", "shell", "template.docx") doc_dirs <- make_doc_dirs(docx_in = docx_in) figures_path <- file.path(here::here(), "OUTPUTS", "figures") tables_path <- file.path(here::here(), "OUTPUTS", "tables") footnotes <- file.path(here::here(), "report", "standard_footnotes.yaml")  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Step 1. # Table addition running add_tables will format and insert tables into the doc. # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- add_tables(   docx_in = doc_dirs$doc_in,   docx_out = doc_dirs$doc_tables,   tables_path = tables_path )  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Step 3. # Next we place in the plots using the add_plots function. # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- add_plots(   docx_in = doc_dirs$doc_tables,   docx_out = doc_dirs$doc_tabs_figs,   figures_path = figures_path ) } # }

what: Updates a Word file to include formatted plots, tables, and footnotes — build_report
title: Updates a Word file to include formatted plots, tables, and footnotes — build_report
text: Reads .docx file returns updated version plots, tables, footnotes replaced.

title: Updates a Word file to include formatted plots, tables, and footnotes — build_report
code: build_report(   docx_in,   docx_out = NULL,   figures_path,   tables_path,   standard_footnotes_yaml = NULL,   add_footnotes = TRUE,   include_object_path = FALSE,   footnotes_fail_on_missing_metadata = TRUE )

title: Updates a Word file to include formatted plots, tables, and footnotes — build_report
text: docx_in Path input .docx update docx_out Path output .docx save figures_path Path images file directory tables_path Path tables file directory standard_footnotes_yaml Path standard_footnotes.yaml report add_footnotes boolean including footnotes document include_object_path boolean including object path footnotes footnotes_fail_on_missing_metadata Boolean allowing objects lack metadata thus footnotes

title: Updates a Word file to include formatted plots, tables, and footnotes — build_report
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Load all dependencies # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- docx_in <- file.path(here::here(), "report", "shell", "template.docx") figures_path <- file.path(here::here(), "OUTPUTS", "figures") tables_path <- file.path(here::here(), "OUTPUTS", "tables") footnotes <- file.path(here::here(), "report", "standard_footnotes.yaml")  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Step 1. # Run the wrapper function to replace figures, tables, and footnotes in a # .docx file. # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- build_report(   docx_in = docx_in,   docx_out = doc_dirs$doc_draft,   figures_path = figures_path,   tables_path = tables_path,   standard_footnotes_yaml = footnotes ) } # }

title: Finalizes the document by removing magic strings and bookmarks — finalize_document
text: docx_in Path input .docx finalize docx_out Path output .docx save

title: Finalizes the document by removing magic strings and bookmarks — finalize_document
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Load all dependencies # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- docx_in <- file.path(here::here(), "report", "shell", "template.docx") figures_path <- file.path(here::here(), "OUTPUTS", "figures") tables_path <- file.path(here::here(), "OUTPUTS", "tables") footnotes <- file.path(here::here(), "report", "standard_footnotes.yaml")  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Step 1. # Run the wrapper function build_report() to replace figures, tables, and # footnotes in a .docx file. # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- build_report(   docx_in = docx_in,   docx_out = doc_dirs$doc_draft,   figures_path = figures_path,   tables_path = tables_path,   standard_footnotes_yaml = footnotes )  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Step 2. # If you are happy with the report and are ready to finalize the document. # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- finalize_document(   docx_in = doc_dirs$doc_draft,   docx_out = doc_dirs$doc_final ) } # }

Additional args used ggsave

title: Removes Bookmarks from a Word file — remove_bookmarks
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Load all dependencies # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- docx_in <- file.path(here::here(), "report", "shell", "template.docx") doc_dirs <- make_doc_dirs(docx_in = docx_in) figures_path <- file.path(here::here(), "OUTPUTS", "figures") tables_path <- file.path(here::here(), "OUTPUTS", "tables") footnotes <- file.path(here::here(), "report", "standard_footnotes.yaml")  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Step 1. # Table addition running add_tables will format and insert tables into the doc. # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- add_tables(   docx_in = docx_in,   docx_out = doc_dirs$doc_tables,   tables_path = tables_path )  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Step 3. # Next we place in the plots using the add_plots function. # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- add_plots(   docx_in = doc_dirs$doc_tables,   docx_out = doc_dirs$doc_tabs_figs,   figures_path = figures_path )  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Step 4. # Now we can add the footnotes to all the inserted figures and tables. # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- add_footnotes(   docx_in = doc_dirs$doc_tabs_figs,   docx_out = doc_dirs$doc_draft,   figures_path = figures_path,   tables_path = tables_path,   footnotes = footnotes )  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Step 5. # Clean the output for a final document creation. This will remove the ties # between reportifyr and the document so be careful! # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- remove_bookmarks(   docx_in = doc_dirs$doc_draft,   docx_out = doc_dirs$doc_final ) } # }

This will remove the ties # between reportifyr and the document so be careful! # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- remove_magic_strings(   docx_in = doc_dirs$doc_draft,   docx_out = doc_dirs$doc_final ) } # }

what: reportifyr: An R package to aid in the drafting of reports. — reportifyr
title: reportifyr: An R package to aid in the drafting of reports. — reportifyr
text: pacakge aims ease table, figure, footnote insertion formatting reports.

title: reportifyr: An R package to aid in the drafting of reports. — reportifyr
text: initialize_report_project: Creates report directory shell, draft, scripts, final subdirectories adds standard_footnotes.yaml /report directory. Initializes python virtual environment subcall initialize_python creates OUTPUTS/figures, OUTPUTS/tables, OUTPUTS/listings directories initialize_python: Creates virtual environment options("venv_dir") set project root otherwise. Also installs python-docx pyyaml packages.

title: reportifyr: An R package to aid in the drafting of reports. — reportifyr
text: ggsave_with_metadata: Wrapper saving ggplot also creates metadata plot object save_rds_with_metadata: Wrapper saveRDS also creates metadata tabular object saves rtf write_csv_with_metadata: Wrapper write.csv also creates metadata tabular object saves rtf save_as_rtf: Saves tabular object (.csv flextable) rtf. called within save_rds_with_metadata write_csv_with_metadata format_flextable: Formats tabular data object flextable simple formatting.

title: reportifyr: An R package to aid in the drafting of reports. — reportifyr
text: write_object_metadata: Creates metadata.json file input object path. Called within analysis output saving functions. update_object_footnotes: Used update footnotes fields within object metadata json files. preview_metadata_files: Generates data frame object metadata within input directory displays object, meta type, equations notes, abbreviations. preview_metadata: Generates metadata data frame singular input file. get_meta_type: Generates meta_type object allow user see available meta_types standard_footnotes.yaml within report directory get_meta_abbrevs: Generates meta_abbrev object allow user see available abbreviations standard_footnotes.yaml within report directory.

title: reportifyr: An R package to aid in the drafting of reports. — reportifyr
text: add_tables: Adds tables word document add_footnotes: Adds footnotes figures tables add_plots: Adds plots word document remove_tables_figures_footnotes: Removes tables, figures, footnotes associated magic string {rpfy}:object.ext remove_magic_strings: Removes magic strings document cutting tie reportifyr producing final document. remove_bookmarks: Removes bookmarks document

title: reportifyr: An R package to aid in the drafting of reports. — reportifyr
text: build_report: Wrapper function remove old tables, figures, footnotes adds new ones. Calls remove_tables_figures_footnotes, make_doc_dirs, add_plots, add_tables, add_footnotes finalize_document: Wrapper function remove magic strings bookmarks document.

title: Wrapper around the saveRDS function. Saves an object as .RDS and .RTF and captures analysis relevant metadata in a .json file — save_rds_with_metadata
text: object R object serialize file connection name file R object saved read meta_type analysis meta type. Defaults "NA" meta_equations Additional equations add metadata meta_notes Additional notes add metadata meta_abbrevs Additional abbrevs add metadata table1_format Boolean declaring object table1 format ... Additional args used saveRDS

title: Wrapper around the saveRDS function. Saves an object as .RDS and .RTF and captures analysis relevant metadata in a .json file — save_rds_with_metadata
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Path to the analysis tables (.RDS) and metadata (.json files) tables.path <- file.path(tempdir(), "tables")  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Save a simple table # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------  out_name <- "01-12345-pk-theoph.csv" save_rds_with_metadata(   object = Theoph,   file = file.path(tables.path, out_name) ) } # }

"" indicates output console. meta_type analysis meta type. Defaults "NA" meta_equations Additional equations metadata meta_notes Additional notes metadata meta_abbrevs Additional abbrevs metadata table1_format Boolean declaring table table1 format ... Additional arguments can passed write.csv

title: Wrapper around the write.csv function. Saves data as .RDS and .RTF and captures analysis relevant metadata in a .json file — write_csv_with_metadata
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{  # Path to the analysis tables (.csv) and metadata (.json files) tables.path <- "OUTPUTS/tables"  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Save a simple table # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------  out_name <- "01-12345-pk-theoph.csv" write_csv_with_metadata(   object = Theoph,   file = file.path(tables.path, out_name), row_names = F ) } # }

title: Writes an object's metadata .json file — write_object_metadata
text: object_file Path file object write metadata meta_type string denoting standard notes use equations Additional equations include metadata, either string single equation vector multiple notes Additional notes include metadata, either string single note vector multiple abbrevs Additional abbreviations include metadata, either string single abbrev vector multiple table1_format Boolean using table1 formatting flextables

title: Writes an object's metadata .json file — write_object_metadata
